mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, causing corporate profit revisions downward and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points within a 3-month window
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing construction activity combined with accelerating real GDP growth confirms robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling period, or the combined proxy correlation exceeds 0.95 indicating redundancy.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply-side capacity utilization approaching full employment, which historically precedes margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases coincide with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential investment reflects collapsing household wealth and credit availability, which suppresses consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive following the trigger condition in over 65% of cases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate hikes and rising unemployment indicate a policy error that stagflates the economy, forcing equity risk premiums higher and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined rate hike and job loss signal yields positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms underlying economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces consumer price inflation, it signals benign supply expansion that supports corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
